observations.recommendation:
1.20 We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for all cash accounts
and immediately submit to the Audit Team the required subsidiary ledgers of the GL
balances of cash accounts for all funds per depository bank; (b) dutifully observe the
prompt preparation and submission of monthly BRS to the audit team, including the
bank statements and valid debit/credit advices, for verification purposes to ascertain
the correctness of the Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements in
compliance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and readily act on reconciling
items for adjustment as appearing in the BRS such as the debit memos, credit memos
and stale checks; and (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i)
reconcile and account for the difference amounting to ₱4,814,483.28 between the
Cashbooks and the Accounting records of the Cash-in-Bank account and thereafter,
record the necessary adjustments; and (ii) conduct regular reconciliation of the
accounts to establish the correctness of both records.

2.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), in coordination with
the Municipal Accountant require the concerned officers and employees to settle their
accounts or refund their unliquidated cash advances at once.

2.11 We recommended further that Management comply with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances.

3.26 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a)
through the Inventory Committee, facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count
of all PPE items and the proper preparation of RPCPPE; (b) require the Municipal
Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and
Property Cards, respectively, in accordance with the format prescribed by MNGAS
and ensure reconciliation of these PPE records.

3.27 After ensuring the integrity of the PPE reports and records through proper
reconciliation and thorough conduct of physical count, we recommended that the
LCE undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. This will ultimately allow for the
recognition of more accurate, realistic, and reliable balances in the PPE accounts.

3.28 Lastly, we recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Accountant to
record all completed Infrastructure Assets and the corresponding Accounts
Payable amounting to ₱43,509,759.99; and (b) henceforth, require the Municipal
Engineering Office (MEO) to submit list or status of work accomplished (SWA) for
all completed projects as at year-end and require the Municipal Accounting Office
(MAO) to recognize all completed projects after conducting independent inspection
based on the list/SWA of completed projects given by the MEO.

4.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal
Accountant to make sure that all ongoing infrastructure projects are accurately
recorded as CIP in the FS at year end; and draw the JEVs taking up the costs of the
on-going infrastructure projects based on actual accomplishments; and (b) ensure the
commitment of the MEO to submit to the MAO a copy of the GPPA Report /SWA
regarding the status or progress of the construction of all on-going projects to
facilitate the timely and accurate recording of CIP in the books.

5.32 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
require: (a) the GSO (all concerned department heads and designated supply officers,
as the case may be) to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of
supply/inventory; and (ii) ensure the preparation and timely submission of Summary
of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Requisition Issue Slip (RSI) to the
Accounting Office; (b) the Accounting Office to (i) prepare and maintain supplies
ledger cards (SLC) for each type of supply/inventory and henceforth, religiously
reconcile this with the stock cards to be maintained by the GSO or concerned
department/unit; and (ii) adopt the perpetual inventory method for all supplies and
materials carried on stock; (c) the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a
comprehensive periodic physical count of ALL inventories in order to establish the
correct balances of the inventory accounts; and (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) resulting therefrom and submit the same to the COA
Office within the prescribed period.

5.33 We also recommended that the LCE consider establishing a central storage
facility to accommodate regular inventory purchases and improve monitoring of
inventory receipt and issuance, in accordance with the perpetual inventory system.

5.34 Lastly, we recommended that the Accounting Office record the
issuance/distribution of the inventories amounting to₱8,353,941.25 and submit a
written explanation on the non-recording of issuance/distribution/consumption of the
said inventories despite submission of consolidated reports of issuances from
requisitioning unit/end-user units.

6.8 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accounting Office to (a) exert best efforts to trace, analyze and reconcile
the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary
adjustments to reflect the balances of the said accounts and furnish the COA of
corrections made; and (b) henceforth, ensure that the balances of the said reciprocal
accounts are reconciled at all times.

7.17 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting
Office to make all necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected
accounts and henceforth follow all the accounting procedures set forth in the NGAS
Manual for LGUs pertaining to setting-up, collection and distribution of Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax. The Accounting Office may start by setting
up the correct RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning
of CY 2025 based on the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of
December 31, 2024 and set up the correct amount of additional collectibles for CY
2025 based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.

7.18 We also recommended that the LCE require the Accounting personnel
handling General Fund and Special Education Fund to regularly reconcile their
records particularly those pertaining to Advance Payments of RPT/SET and
Discounts thereof.

8.6 We reiterated our recommendation that (a) the Municipal Accounting Office
(i) exert best efforts to determine the composition of the unidentified balances
recorded in the Due to LGUs – TF and Due to NGAs accounts (both GF and TF); and
(ii) draw the appropriate entry to derecognize the liability to DSWD amounting to the
₱674,981.00 representing return of grants of SLP members, this amount should be
transferred to the SLP Association/s; (iii) submit documents to support the adjusting
entry made in the Due to NGAs – GF account amounting to ₱990,000.00; (iv) draw the
appropriate correcting entries for all the funds received incorrectly classified as
Due to NGAs; and (b) the Accountant of Mexico Community Hospital prioritize the
immediate completion of the liquidation documents needed to record the utilization
and liquidation of the 2018 MHCAP and 2019 Kabayan Partylist Assistance from
DOH.

9.7 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to: (a)
ensure that all adjustments recorded in the books are properly supported by
including the supporting evidences and traces of records of the transactions under
Prior Period Adjustments in the entries recorded in Cash Disbursement Journals and
Journal Entry Vouchers, such as original entry, computation, to substantiate the
adjustments; and (b) reflect disclosure of Prior Period Adjustments in the Notes to
Financial Statements in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting
Standards 3 or Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors

We recommended that the Departments concern Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office to make all necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts and henceforth, follow all the accounting procedures set forth in the NGAS Manual for LGUS pertaining to setting-up, collections and distribution of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax. The Accounting Office may start by setting up the correct RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of CY 2023 based on the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2022 and establish the correct amount of additional collectibles for CY 2023 based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.
We also recommended that Management enjoin other concerned departments to assist the Accounting Office in implementing said recommendations, specifically the Municipal Assessor’s Office to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the assessment rolls on or before the 31st of December each year and based thereon, at the beginning of every year, the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
observations.managementAction: already held discussions and are in the process of adopting the Modified Accrual Basis of Accounting for Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET).

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to (a) coordinate with each other and reconcile their records to ascertain the correctness of the Land account balance; and (b) facilitate the registration in the name of the Municipal Government the parcel of lots acquired which were not yet covered by OCTs/TCTs to preclude third party claims over said properties.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) submit all necessary documents that will justify the derecognition of the amount of ₱858,523.38 from the Land account balance; and (b) record in the books of accounts all unrecorded parcels of land acquired by the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution and Deed of Donation to prove the claim that the ownership over the parcel of land located at Barangay San Jose Malino embraced by TCT No. 042-2015012952 was already transferred to the Department of Education (DepED).
observations.managementAction: Coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal Assessor’s Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ascertain the correctness of the Land account balance. The Municipality is still in the process of gathering necessary documents pertaining to the derecognized Land. No action taken.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the (a) LCE create an Inventory Committee (IC) to facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count of PPE in order to present a realistic balance of the PPE accounts in the financial statements; (b) Municipal Treasurer maintain property cards for PPE; and (c) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the correcting entries to reflect the accurate carrying amount of the affected PPE items, in accordance with Item 42 of PPSAS No. 3; and (ii) record in the book of accounts all unrecorded lots donated to the Municipal Government of Mexico.
We also recommended that Management work on the eventual disposal of the eight unserviceable motor vehicles and the subsequently derecognition from the books of accounts, in pursuance with the provision of Section 82 of IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already created its Inventory Committee in November 2023 but failed to conduct physical count of PPE. The Municipality has already created its General Services Office in June 2023 but maintenance of property cards is still for implementation. The recording in its books of the 26 donated lots was left unprioritized. No action was taken by the Municipality to dispose the eight (8) unserviceable motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the MENRO to revisit the provisions of Tripartite MOA for any possible revisions, amendments or addendum, after the Municipality subsidizes the whole septage fees, to consider (i) reducing the share of Sinukuan Water System, Inc.; (ii) giving of Municipal funds directly to a private desludging company without passing it through the water utility company; and (iii) determine the lowest possible cost of desludging services of a private desludging company through public bidding.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) BAC to conduct public bidding to obtain the lowest possible cost of desludging services in accordance with RA 9184; and (b) Acting Municipal Accountant to require the Sinukuan Water System, Inc. and Soliman E.C. Septic Tank Disposal for the submission of their respective liquidation vouchers and supporting documents such as Desludging Job Order slips and monthly completion list which includes the detailed information of water subscribers who availed of desludging services in order to verify if the Municipal funds were entirely used to its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ceased the payment of 100% financial subsidy for residential septage fees to Sinukuan Water System, Inc. and suspended its plan to continue the provision of subsidy for septages fees to its constituents. The Municipality is currently monitoring the on-going desludging services being performed by Soliman E.C Septic Tank Disposal to ensure that the amount already paid to Sinukuan Water System, Inc. will be fully exhausted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) investigate the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account to determine if these are valid obligations of the LGU and immediately remit the same if so warranted; (ii) trace the prior years’ accounting entries pertaining to the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and subsequently prepare corresponding correcting/adjusting entries, if necessary; and (iii) strictly adhere to relevant laws, rules, and regulations concerning the accurate withholding of taxes and contributions, ensuring their timely and complete remittance.
observations.managementAction: No specific remedial action furnished in the report other than acknowledgement of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances and charges within the prescribed period, pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts of the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office submit the schedule of payments for the partially settled CYs 2013 and 2014 disallowed payment of PEI to facilitate the issuance of the Notice of Settlement of Suspension/ Disallowances/ Charges.
observations.managementAction: No action detailed in the section aside from the recommendation to enforce settlement and submission of schedule of payments.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to :
(a) require the Inventory Committee (i) to facilitate the conduct of a thorough
physical count of all PPE items and the preparation of RPCPPE; and (ii) submit to
the Audit Team the PIF and henceforth abide by the provisions of COA Circular
2018-002 dated May 31, 2018; and (b) require the Acting Municipal Accountant and
Municipal General Services Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property
Cards, respectively, in accordance with the format prescribed by MNGAS and ensure
reconciliation of these PPE records.

1.12 Moreover, we recommended and Management also agreed to consider the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 to finally be able to reflect a realistic and reliable balances of the
PPE accounts.

3.27 We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the: (a) PCF
Custodian to (i) record immediately all PCF received and disbursements made out of
said fund to monitor the PCF balance; (ii) immediately record and update posting of
transactions in the Petty Cash Disbursement Record, and must see to it that actual
cash on hand reconciles with the balance per PCF Disbursement record at the end of
the day; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) reassess the reasonable amount of PCF, if
excessive, decrease the PCF of the AO to a level sufficient to meet the petty expenses
in a month; and (ii) replenishment must be done when disbursement reaches 75% of
the total PCF, in time for the release of the next replenishment, or once a month
depending on the PCF balance at hand; (c) Accountant to regularly review the
accountability of the PCF Custodian and apply the appropriate fidelity bond based
on actual cash accountability in accordance with the Treasury Circular No. 11-2021
dated April 26, 2021; (d) instruct the PCF Custodian and Municipal Treasurer to
refrain from using the PCF for regular expenses. Instead, seek approval from the
Sangguniang Bayan to establish a special cash advance-operating expenses to include
gasoline expenses which are not possible for Purchase Order with gasoline stations.

3.28 Moreover, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the
Management agreed that the Acting – Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the
concerned Barangays Treasurers for the (a) turn-over of the CTC booklets issued to
them or (b) remittance of the collections made by issuing the said AFs, if any, and
henceforth closely monitor all accountable forms issued to Barangays.

3.29 The Municipal Treasurer informed the Audit Team that for the Petty Cash Fund,
the excess from the ₱300,000.00 will be liquidated and a new cash advance of ₱150,000.00
will be requested for it to be sufficient for the recurring petty expenses of the municipality
in a month and in order to fit in the current maximum cash accountability of the fidelity
bond of the petty cash officer.

3.30 Moreso, for the Special Cash Advance, MTO is still in the process of drafting a
request for the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

4.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to (a) exert best efforts to trace, analyze and reconcile the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjustments
to reflect the balances of the said accounts and furnish the COA of corrections made;
and (b) henceforth, ensure that the balances of the said reciprocal accounts are
reconciled at all times.

6.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure the optimum and the
efficient utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality through proper
planning and efficient project implementation and instruct the Municipal Engineer
and MPDO to strictly monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the
projects under the 20% DF so that necessary and timely intervention can be made to
ensure that the timeliness for the implementation of programmed projects are met.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit the detailed schedule of AP-GF and Accrual Set-up on AP- MCH amounting to ₱35,487,985.69 and ₱2,889,915.46, respectively, as of December 31, 2022; and, henceforth, regularly maintain the detailed schedule of AP, which shows the name of suppliers or payee, nature of transactions, total amount of unpaid goods or services, and aged of outstanding accounts payable per supplier or payee.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Accountant II of MCH to investigate the ₱802,429.96 discrepancies and, henceforth, regularly reconcile the balances of AP-MCH with Detailed Schedule of Unpaid Medicines and Medical Supplies.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that (a) the Municipal Accounting Office (i) exert best efforts to determine the composition of the unidentified balances recorded in the Due to LGUs – TF and Due to NGAs accounts (both GF and TF); and (ii) draw the appropriate entry to derecognize the liability to the DSWD amounting to the ₱674,981.00 representing return of grants of SLP members, this amount should be transferred to the SLP Association/s; and (b) the Accountant of Mexico Community Hospital prioritize the immediate completion of the liquidation documents needed to record the utilization and liquidation of the 2018 MHCAP and 2019 Kabayan Partylist Assistance from DOH.
Further, we recommended that the (a) the Municipal Accounting Office draw the appropriate entries to (i) transfer the excess/unexpended balances of several DOLE DILEEP LGU Counterpart back to the General Fund; (ii) record the taxes withheld from One COVID-19 allowances, cause the immediate remittance thereof to the BIR and furnish the Audit Team of the proof of payment to BIR; and (iii) submit documents to support the adjusting entry made in the Due to NGAs – GF account amounting to ₱990,000.00.
observations.managementAction:
On-going investigation to determine the composition of the unidentified balances of the accounts.
The Municipality coordinated with the DSWD regarding the return of grants of SLP members and is waiting for specific instructions therefrom.
The MCH is still in the process of completing the liquidation documents needed to record the utilization and liquidation of the 2018 MHCAP and 2019 Kabayan Partylist Assistance from DOH.
Necessary entries have been recorded by the Municipal Accounting Office accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), and Municipal Engineer to strengthen the cycle of planning, execution, monitoring and evaluation of projects for prioritization under the 20% DF in order to ensure that only well-prepared and implementation-ready projects are included in the budget for 20% DF.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to require the submission of Program of Works and Vicinity Map by the Municipal Engineer for every infrastructure project before the finalization of approved budget in the next budget year to prevent the incurrence of significant unobligated balance.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (i) Acting Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Engineer, to submit the plans, pictures of billboard and signboard installed at the project site; and (ii) Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to ensure that only the latest available Financial Statement of the contractor will be used in computing Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC).
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Engineer have transmitted the project pictures of billboard and signboard for the projects concerned.
The BAC ensured use of latest financial statements in computing NFCC.

observations.recommendation: We once again commended the Management for its compliance with the applicable rules and regulations in the grant and utilization of fund transfers received from other government agencies and for the timely implementation of the projects charged thereto. Meanwhile, we recommended that the Management (a) require the concerned officials to submit explanations or pertinent documents with respect to projects lacking complete documentation such as (i) PESO on the proof of payment to SSS of Informal Sector Workers-Participants SSS contribution; (ii) Engineering Office on the lacking contractor’s affidavit on payment of laborers and materials; (iii) MSWDO on the lacking distribution list for the Supplemental Feeding; and (iv) MHO on the lacking Attestation Form and COVID-19 Risk Exposure Classification Scorecards of COVID-19 Allowance recipients; (b) require the Accounting Unit to ensure the completeness of documentary requirements before processing payments and religiously refer to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for common documentary requirements; and (c) require the Engineering Office to abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and strictly demand from contractors the required design and format of project signboard for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for complying with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the (a) appropriation of not less than five percent (5%) of the LGU’s estimated revenue from regular sources for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF);(b) setting aside 30% of the LDRRMF to the Quick Response Fund (QRF); (c) transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund (STF); (d) creation of a Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC); (e) establishment of a Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO); (f) strengthening the capacity of the LDRRMO; and (g) preparation and submission of the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
However, we recommended that Management (a) increase its effort in maximizing the use of the LDRRMF by targeting the full implementation of programmed PPAs to expand its capability and responsiveness in addressing disaster risk reduction and management and climate adversities; and (b) ensure the immediate transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund (STF) to ensure that the balance of the Trust Liability – DRRM account reflected in the financial statements as of year- end is accurate and reliable.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We however, recommended a revised Executive Order be issued for the reorganization to update and exclude the already retired members.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order for the revision and updating of GAD Focal Point System already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) HR department to ensure that JO employees are paid on a daily basis; and (b) Accounting department to ensure that accomplishment reports are attached to the monthly reports of cash disbursements as necessitated by COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the (a) Hospital Consignment Committee of the MCH, if any, or its Medical Management to submit an explanation on the non-invitation to submit price offers to various suppliers/manufacturers; (b) BAC to submit an explanation as to the absence of other required documents in the eligibility determination of the consignors; (c) Accounting Unit of the MCH to submit explanation on the absence of prescription or any other pertinent reports/records to support the monthly consigned consumption of consigned drugs and medicines and medical supplies.
Henceforth, we also recommended that the LCE require the Hospital Consignment Committee, if any, or its Medical Management and the BAC to strictly observe and comply with the specific guidelines and procedures in the procurement of consigned drugs and medicines prescribed under DOH Administrative Order No. 2006-0039 for transparency, competitiveness, equity, efficiency and economy in its consignment system.
observations.managementAction: Explanation rendered by the MCH only centered around the discussion that they have only followed the process that was in place in the past. No formal explanation has been offered by the Bids and Awards Committee but it assured the Audit Team that it will strictly observe and comply with the specific guidelines and procedures in the procurement of consigned drugs and medicines prescribed under DOH Administrative Order No. 2006-0039, moving forward.

observations.recommendation:
1.16. We commended the Municipality for generally abiding by the rules and
regulations on the (a) provision of funds for COVID-19-related PPAs under LBC No.
124 and DILG – DBM JMC No. 01; (b) procurement set forth in RA 9184; and (c)
documentary requirements per COA Circular No. 2012-001.
1.16. However, we recommended that the Municipality be abreast with and follow
the latest guidelines being issued by concerned government agencies and
instrumentalities pertaining to COVID-19 mitigation to prevent situations similar
with what transpired during the procurement of COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary
supplies and services that might result in wastage of funds to the detriment of the
government. Further, we recommended that the Municipality, henceforth, practice
the diligence in the use of government funds.

2.28 We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE direct all departments with
inventories under their custody to (a) install and execute control procedures to
monitor all inventories procured and received under their custody; (b) ensure the
preparation and timely submission of reports pertaining to the issuance of inventory
items; and (c) strictly comply with the rules and regulations contained in the MNGAS,
specifically the preparation of the required inventory reports (stock cards).
2.29 We also reiterated our recommendations that the LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant to instruct the personnel in-charge in the recording of inventories to (a)
prepare and maintain the supplies ledger cards for all inventories and henceforth
strictly reconcile said supplies ledger cards with the stock cards; and (b) make the
necessary adjusting entries called for based on the deficiencies noted.
2.30 Further, we recommended that the LCE (a) create an Inventory Committee to
facilitate the pressing need of conducting a physical count of all inventories in order
to establish the correct balances of the accounts and henceforth, religiously conduct
63
the same, and prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories
(RCPI); (b) require all departments to submit to the physical count to be conducted
by the Inventory Committee, otherwise accountability and responsibility for the
amount corresponding to the costs of inexistent or unaccounted supplies shall be
established; (c) require the Accounting Office to set up the correct balances of the
inventory accounts based on the physical count of inventory to be facilitated by the
Inventory Committee and henceforth, abide by the provisions of the MNGAS on the
proper accounting of Inventory.

3.12 We reiterated our previous recommendation that the LCE create an Inventory
Committee to facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count of PPE in order to
present a realistic balance of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
3.13 Further, we recommended that the (i) Municipal Treasurer maintain property
cards for PPE, and the (ii) Municipal Accountant (a) revisit the provisions of PPSAS
Nos. 3 and 7; (c) adjust the accumulated depreciation of PPE items purchased prior
to CY 2016 to reflect their correct carrying amount in the books of accounts; and (c)
take up in the books all unrecorded road lots donated to the Municipality.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each
other and reconcile their records to ascertain the correctness of the Land account
balance and facilitate the registration, in the name of the Municipal Government, of
the parcel of lots acquired which were not yet covered by TCTs to preclude third
party claims over said properties; and (b) the Accounting Office to record in the
books of accounts all unrecorded parcels of land acquired by the Municipality.

5.4 We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accounting Office (a)
exert best efforts to determine the composition of the unidentified balances recorded
in the Due to LGUs – TF and Due to NGAs accounts (both GF and TF); (b) draw the
appropriate entry to derecognize the 2019 cash incentive – SCFLG from the PGP
amounting to ₱100,000.00 as liability and record the same income; (c) draw the
appropriate entry to derecognize the liability to DSWD amounting to the ₱674,981.00
representing return of grants of SLP members, this amount should be transferred to
the SLP Association/s.
5.5 Further, we recommended that the (a) Accountant of Mexico Community
Hospital prioritize the immediate completion of the liquidation documents needed to
record the utilization and liquidation of the 2018 MHCAP and 2019 Kabayan
Partylist Assistance from DOH; (b) Municipal Public Employment Service Office
(PESO) immediately submit to the Accounting Office the documents pertaining to the
distribution of 53 sewing machines charged to the 2021 DOLE DILEEP Kabuhayan
granted on May 28, 2021; and (c) Municipal Accounting Office draw the appropriate
entries to (i) de-recognize the cash assistance for the ideal functionality of LCPC
amounting to ₱100,000.00 and record it as income; and (ii) correct the erroneous
entry made representing return of unutilized performance-based grant to DOH
amounting to ₱50,000.00.

12.11 We recommended that the Local School Board (a) continue to utilize the
Special Education Fund only for purposes explicitly allowed in DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 taking into consideration the latest guidelines being
issued on the utilization of Special Education Fund (SEF).
12.12 We also recommended that the Accounting Office correct the Report of SEF
Utilization for CY 2021 for re-submission to DepEd Central Office (CO), copy
furnished the Sangguniang Bayan (SB); Regional Offices of DBM and DILG; and our
office.

7.10 Meanwhile, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Acting
Municipal Treasurer to immediately (i) return/refund of the unused funds from the
Financial Assistance for LGUs affected by typhoon Ulysses in the amount of
₱1,120.00; and (ii) transfer back to the General Fund the LGU Counterparts of the
DOLE DILEEP Kabuhayan Program totaling ₱94,948.67; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to record the return/transfer of the funds accordingly.

8.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC)
to adopt measures for consistent optimum utilization of the 20% DF within the budget
year.
8.14 We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to
thoroughly review the Vicinity Map of Original Plans and indicate thereof the exact
project site for inspection and audit purposes.

9.17 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Council (MDRRMC) (i) conduct a thorough evaluation and planning of
the DRRM PPAs for effective and efficient utilization of the LDRRMF, (ii) include
the activities to be funded from the unexpended DRRMF in the LDRRMFIP; (b)
Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) ensure the revision of the
Annual Investment Program (AIP) for the inclusion of new expenditure items which
are to be charged against the DRRMF unexpended balance; and (c) Municipal Budget
Officer (MBO) prepare and maintain separate Registries of Appropriations,

possible, request other Barangays with MRF for the clustering scheme as allowed under

Officer, provide the required funding to help the Barangays in establishing their own

the appropriate journal entries to correct the accounting errors committed; and (c)

the correct amount of taxes and the timely and full remittance thereof.

settlement of the audit disallowances and charges within the prescribed period,
pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts of
the Commission on Audit.

schedule of payments for the partially settled CYs 2013 and 2014 disallowed payment

inventory reports.

based on the foregoing deficiencies noted.

Officer maintain for each class of PPE a Property Card for the recording of PPE with
information as to its description, custody and disposal; (c) Local Chief Executive create an

physical count of the property and equipment of the Municipality in order to present a
realistic balance of property accounts and make a fair presentation of accounts in the
financial statements.

transfer the ownership of all assets, including those acquired in the previous years,
issued to other government entities.

and evaluation of programs and projects for prioritization under the 20% DF in order

continuously provide new and improved services to the Municipal’s constituents and
the public.

13.9. However, we recommended that the Management (a) continuously exert
efforts for the completion of the sex-disaggregated GAD database; and (b) perform

manpower to the MENRO to ensure the regular monitoring of the implementation of
SWM-related projects and to aid in the strict imposition of fines and penalties against
violators of prohibited acts; and (b) improve the Municipality’s compliance with the
provision of Republic Act No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of
2000 particularly on the proper segregation and disposal of wastes.

13.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), as Chairman of the
LSB, the Co-Chairman, and other members of the LSB (a) properly plan the PPAs
to be implemented from the fund; and (b) maximize the utilization of the SEF to
attain its objectives.

14.18. However, we recommended that Management consider reviewing its
forecasting system and its collection strategies on revenue accounts with reported
decreases.
14.19. We also recommended that Management act upon the enforcement of its
Market Code through the (a) execution of a Lease Contract Agreements with each
stallholder of the Municipal Public Market, with the obligations and rights of both
parties clearly defined and with fair and equitable terms to both parties; and (b)
prioritization of the immediate establishment of the Public Market Management
Office to improve the administration of the Public Market.

15.9. However, we still recommended that Management, through the Human
Resource Management Office, continue to evaluate and review the Municipality’s
organizational structure, functions, systems and procedures and staffing to
determine the appropriate human resource complement for the agency.

16.7. We recommended that Management strictly enforce the settlement of the
audit disallowances and charges within the prescribed period, pursuant to the 2009
Revised Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts of the Commission on
Audit.

17.8. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a
thorough analysis of the due to BIR account to determine the composition of the
ending balance.

18.7. We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant (a) conduct
monthly reconciliation between the recognized collected premiums and loan
amortization payments and the remittances made; and (b) investigate the reported
balances and determine if these are valid obligations of the Municipality.

19.12. However, we recommended that the Management warrant the optimal
utilization of the 5% GAD in order to ensure the integration of gender equality
principles and perspectives in the LGU.

20.7. In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that Management
require all concerned offices to furnish the Audit Team with copy of the purchase
orders and contract documents not later than five working days from the issuance
of the purchase orders and perfections of contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents.

21.5. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly
observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 particularly the (a) posting of ITB, COA,
Contract/PO and NTP; (b) invitation of observers in all stages of the procurement
process; (c) preparation and submission of the PERT/CPM network; and (d)
submission of DOLE Approved Construction Safety and Health Program of
bidders/contractors.
21.6. We also recommended that Management submit a written explanation on the
non-provision of the contractor of complete personal protective equipment of
laborers during the project duration despite inclusion thereof in the total contract
cost.

22.22. We recommended that Management (a) secure copy of the Deed of Donation
from the donor to establish authority from the use of the lot where the project was
constructed; and (b) closely monitor the implementation of the projects “Construction
of covered court at St. Joseph’s Subdivision” and “Upgrading and concreting of road
at San Patricio and enforce the collection of liquidated damages from the contractor.

22.23. Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the
Municipal Engineer, require the Contractor concerned to cause the repair of the
portion of the project found to be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality.

23.10. For the noted observation, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive,
in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and implement guidelines for the
grant of reasonable amount of mobile allowance to municipal officials, taking into
consideration the current promos being offered by network providers.

(b) require the maintenance and keeping of the required property records pursuant to the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs.

We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Mexico Community Hospital’s (MCH) officials to submit to the Accounting Department the records and documents of all equipment donated to the MCH.

Moreover, we recommended that management refer to National Budget Circular No. 425 dated January 28, 1992 or the Manual on the Disposal of Government Property for the proper disposal of the Municipality’s unserviceable assets.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer agreed to (a) coordinate with the DA in identifying priority locations of FMRs taking into account the number of farmers who shall benefit from the project, the kind, amount and importance of agriculture products in the area; and (b) ensure that the FMRs to be implemented address the conveyance of food/ agricultural/aquatic farm produce and services and that projects to be undertaken are located within the key production areas or marginal lands which link these areas to major markets, post-harvest facilities, and processing zones/industries.

conduct regular field monitoring on the progress and development of the implementation of various projects; and
provide overall management and guidance in the project monitoring during the implementation, from the detailed engineering, pre-construction and construction stages to ensure timely project implementation and completion.
require the supplier to submit the Delivery Receipts detailing the number and description of items received and duly signed by the authorized receiving personnel, otherwise the amount paid for the supply and delivery of construction materials in CYs 2016 and 2017 will be suspended in audit.

₱77,250.00 pertaining to items paid such as excavation, landscaping, lettering, and plumbing works because they are not deliverables, but scope of works that requires manpower for their completion.

We recommended that the GFPS:
check that GAD planning and budgeting is harmoniously carried out by the different offices/departments;
initiate the capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool to guarantee the integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the agency programs and projects and to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and
continue to strengthen the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) in accordance with the afore-cited rules and regulations to ensure the mainstreaming of GAD in the systems and processes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality through the LCPC improve compliance to the provisions of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 specifically on the appropriation of LCPC budget and preparation of required reports.

We recommended and the that Local Chief Executive agreed to:
instruct the HR department (i) to check the performance of the BTR machines and the accuracy of produced reports from the BTR system to fully utilize and maximize its benefits; (ii)  to require the preparation of individual accomplishment reports to facilitate the continuous evaluation of the JOs performances; (iii) to install internal control on the hiring and supervision/management of all personnel; and (iv) refer to the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40 series of 1998 for the policy guidelines for contract of services; and
require the Accounting Office to strengthen internal control over processing of payroll and liquidation reports by checking the sufficiency of the supporting documents for each and every type of transactions.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to instruct the Accounting personnel in charge of payroll and BIR remittances to (a) comply with pertinent rules and regulations pertaining to the withholding of taxes for income payments of employees under job order contracts; and (b) observe the timely remittance of the taxes withheld.

We also recommended that the LSB:
come up with a strategic prioritization plan in the allocation of the SEF to public schools taking into consideration the most important needs of the students and not just for repairs of school facilities for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; and
prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

(b) undertake an intensive information campaign to involve the general public in the cleanliness and beautification projects of the locality and conduct regular monitoring on the status of implementation of the PPAs relevant to solid waste management; and
